keynotes.name: Dr. Bharath Ramsundar
keynotes.role: Founder & CEO, Deep Forest Sciences; Lead Maintainer, DeepChem
keynotes.bio: Bharath Ramsundar, PhD, is founder and CEO of Deep Forest Sciences and lead maintainer of the DeepChem open-source consortium for medicine discovery. He previously co-founded Computable and holds a PhD in Computer Science from Stanford University. His work focuses on deep learning applications in drug discovery.

keynotes.name: Dr. Nikunj Oza
keynotes.role: Leader, Data Sciences Group, NASA Ames Research Center
keynotes.bio: Nikunj Oza leads the Data Sciences Group at NASA Ames and a NASA project team applying machine learning to aviation safety and operations. His research spans data mining, machine learning, ensemble methods, anomaly detection, and aerospace applications. He has published 50+ research papers and received multiple NASA honor awards.

keynotes.name: Ashish Bansal
keynotes.role: ML Infrastructure Leader, Google; Author of Advanced NLP
keynotes.bio: Ashish works on accelerating ML workloads at Google. He has previously worked on recommendation systems and NLP at Amazon/Twitch, Twitter, and Capital One, and has founded startups. He is co-founder of the IIT AI/ML forum and, in this keynote, presented key architectural foundations behind LLM development and future directions.

keynotes.name: Robert Ober
keynotes.role: Chief Platform Architect for Data Center Products, NVIDIA
keynotes.bio: Rob is NVIDIA's Chief Platform Architect for data center products. He works with global hyperscales to architect AI clusters and datacenters, systems and platform architecture, and influence NVIDIA silicon, systems, and software roadmaps. His background is in processor and system architecture with experience in storage, networking, and wireless.

keynotes.name: Surbhi Kaul
keynotes.role: Product and Engineering Leader (Google, YouTube, Netflix, Cisco)
keynotes.bio: With 20 years of experience leading product design, development, and strategy in Fortune 500 companies, Surbhi has built and led product and engineering teams at Google, YouTube, Netflix, and Cisco. Over the last decade she launched multiple AI and machine-learning-driven products, including query disambiguation and speech recognition efforts for Google Home and Google Assistant devices.

keynotes.name: Dr. Jan Heng
keynotes.role: Assistant Professor of Pathology, Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center (Harvard Medical School)
keynotes.bio: Dr. Jan Heng is an Assistant Professor of Pathology at Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center (BIDMC), Harvard Medical School, Boston. She is a translational biomedical scientist with expertise in reproductive health and cancer biology. She began her career in pregnancy biomarker research with a PhD from the University of Melbourne, and later continued her work as a post-doctoral fellow at the University of Toronto. She transitioned to breast cancer research at BIDMC and has applied AI methods to breast cancer research for the past 10 years.
